name: Japanese Tea Garden
summary_en: The Japanese Tea Garden appears chiefly in Golden Gate Park planning documents. A 2002 EIR for the Concourse Authority projects lists the garden as a contributing feature of the proposed park historic district and notes it "has been determined eligible for listing on the California Register of Historical Resources," concluding the project would not materially alter it. Revisions to the New de Young Museum EIR (2000-2001) confirm the garden is "individually eligible for listing on the National Register of Historic Places" and analyze the museum's effect on it, including a proposed tower visible from the garden. It is operated by the Recreation and Park Department, whose FY2025 budget line is about $250M; no contract in the data names it.
